The workaround lets homegrown platforms build a domestic alternative to Polymarket, which cannot operate locally, while keeping payouts inside Japan's established points economy rather than crypto or cash settlement.

title: Crypto exchange BitMEX removes CEO, CFO and head of growth
blurb: BitMEX removed CEO Stephan Lutz, CFO Ina Steiner, and chief growth officer Raphael Polansky, installing global general counsel Peter Wilkinson as chief executive. The exchange, co-founded in 2014 and reportedly hunting for a buyer, is cutting executive costs as a prolonged downturn in digital asset prices pressures the industry.

title: South Korea’s $518 billion AI chip push shows crypto is still losing the capital race
blurb: Samsung and SK Hynix committed roughly $518 billion to four new South Korean chip fabs to double national DRAM output within five years, pulling their AI memory timeline forward by about a decade. The buildout is part of the same capital cycle that has pressured crypto all year, coinciding with record US spot bitcoin ETF outflows, weaker prices, and miners pivoting to AI hosting.

title: Bitcoin falls below $60,000, on track for a rare back-to-back quarterly loss
blurb: Bitcoin slipped below $60,000 over the weekend and is on track to close the second quarter down roughly 12%, following a 22% first-quarter drop that makes this only the third back-to-back losing first half in its history. The slide, driven by US spot bitcoin ETF outflows, a hawkish Fed under Chair Kevin Warsh, and a dollar near a seven-month high, hit altcoins harder, with ether down about 25% on the quarter.

title: Bitcoin clings to key support level as weekly US spot ETF outflows hit $1.8B and Fed rate hike bets mount: analysts
blurb: Bitcoin is trading near $60,000, holding just above a key support level after US spot BTC ETFs shed $1.79B last week, one of the largest weekly outflows on record. Options desks report risk reversals skewed toward puts and implied volatility grinding higher, with rate markets now pricing roughly 1.5 additional Fed hikes versus the two cuts expected before the Middle East conflict.

title: Polymarket hack updated to $3.1 million days after the platform promised users full refunds
blurb: Hackers stole roughly $3.1 million in Polymarket's PUSD token from 11 user wallets after a compromised third-party vendor injected a malicious script into the platform's frontend, with funds bridged from Polygon to Ethereum. The phishing breach lands as Polymarket faces a federal investigation over allegedly deceptive social media promotions, compounding a string of recent security incidents at the prediction-markets platform.

title: $4 billion gone. Spot bitcoin ETFs are on track for their worst month on record
blurb: U.S.-listed spot bitcoin ETFs have logged $4.06 billion in net outflows in June, the largest monthly redemption since the products launched in January 2024 and well above the prior $3.56 billion record set in February 2025. The retreat extends a $6.5 billion two-month exodus and tracks bitcoin's roughly 30% first-half decline, signaling that the institutional demand these regulated wrappers were built to channel has sharply reversed.

title: SBI's $289 million Bitbank deal is symptomatic of Japan's crypto consolidation: Architect Partners
blurb: SBI Holdings agreed to acquire Japanese crypto exchange Bitbank for $289 million, doubling its assets under custody to roughly 1.1 trillion yen and adding nearly 1 million accounts as tougher rules raise the cost of running standalone exchanges. Architect Partners frames the deal as a bet on regulated scale over earnings and expects consolidation to continue, naming private-equity-owned bitFlyer as the next likely target.

title: CZ wants to make the U.S. the 'capital of crypto': State of Crypto
blurb: Binance founder Changpeng Zhao said in two CoinDesk interviews that he wants to strengthen the U.S. crypto market, including having Binance.US tap Binance Global's liquidity, while maintaining he no longer plans to run an exchange directly. Zhao, who served four months in 2024 for Bank Secrecy Act violations, remains majority shareholder of both Binance and Binance.US and frames his current role as an informal adviser to portfolio companies.

title: South Korea’s Kiwoom Securities to acquire stake in Bithumb crypto exchange: report
blurb: Kiwoom Securities, a major South Korean brokerage, is in talks to buy a stake in Bithumb through a third-party share issuance, the second-largest local crypto exchange. The move follows Hana Bank's $670M Dunamu stake and Samsung subsidiaries' $407.7M purchase, as institutions position ahead of the Digital Asset Basic Act, which may cap single-shareholder ownership at 20% with exceptions up to 34%.

title: SoFi Launches SOFIUSD Stablecoin on Solana
blurb: SoFi launched SOFIUSD, a dollar-backed stablecoin issued on Solana, extending its national bank charter into onchain settlement. The move lets SoFi route payments and stored value across a public blockchain rather than relying solely on ACH and card rails, and positions a chartered bank to compete directly with issuers like Circle on cross-border and programmable-money use cases.

title: AI boom risks global financial crash, warn central bankers
blurb: Central bankers warned that surging AI valuations and concentrated investment could trigger a global financial crash if the boom unwinds, echoing concerns about market exposure to a handful of richly valued tech and infrastructure names. The warning signals that regulators are starting to treat AI capital flows as a systemic-risk question, which could shape how banks and investors are scrutinized for their exposure to the sector.

title: Amazon’s Rapid Delivery Push Triggers $15 Billion Rout for Eternal, Swiggy
blurb: Amazon and Walmart's Flipkart are moving into India's 10-minute delivery market, the segment Eternal and Swiggy built, triggering a $15B selloff across the two incumbents' shares. The entry of well-capitalized e-commerce giants threatens the unit economics of quick commerce, where thin margins already depend on dense order volume and tight delivery-cost control.

title: Crypto exchange BitMEX removes CEO, CFO and head of growth
blurb: BitMEX removed CEO Stephan Lutz, CFO Ina Steiner, and chief growth officer Raphael Polansky, with global general counsel Peter Wilkinson stepping in as CEO. The clearout comes as the 2014-founded exchange, which pleaded guilty to U.S. anti-money-laundering charges in 2020, reportedly hunts for a buyer and trims costs amid the latest crypto downturn.

title: Michael Saylor signals another bitcoin buy as Strategy sits about $13 billion underwater
blurb: Michael Saylor signaled another bitcoin purchase by Strategy, which would mark a fourth straight week of buying even as its 847,363 BTC position sits roughly $13 billion underwater at a $75,646 average cost. The firm's enterprise mNAV slipped below 1 for the first time as MSTR hit a February 2024 low and its STRC preferred fell to a record, making capital raises through new share sales harder.

title: Japan’s Prediction Market Startups Use Loyalty Points to Bypass Gambling Ban
blurb: Japanese prediction-market startups, barred from real-money betting under the country's gambling laws, are routing payouts through retail loyalty points and shopping vouchers to build a domestic alternative to Polymarket. The workaround sidesteps regulatory exposure by keeping settlement in points rather than cash, though it caps liquidity and leaves these platforms a structural step behind crypto-settled rivals operating offshore.

title: PBOC’s New Overnight Rate Said Below Forecasts in Hint at Easing
blurb: The PBOC set the rate on its new overnight liquidity tool below market expectations, a move economists read as a de facto rate cut aimed at lowering short-term borrowing costs. The easing signal lands as China's central bank leans on a fresh tool to manage market liquidity, suggesting a more accommodative stance that could ripple through interbank funding rates and the yuan.

title: Bitcoin clings to key support level as weekly US spot ETF outflows hit $1.8B and Fed rate hike bets mount: analysts
blurb: Bitcoin is trading around $60,000, just above the support level analysts are watching, after U.S. spot BTC ETFs shed $1.79B last week in one of the largest weekly net outflows on record. Rate markets have swung to pricing roughly 1.5 additional Fed hikes from two cuts a month ago, and options desks report risk reversals skewed toward puts as traders pay up for downside protection into July expiry.
